user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You paused your meal-kit subscription a while ago while you were traveling, and now you're back and want to RESUME it -- turn deliveries back on. The subscription already exists; it's just paused. You do NOT want to change anything else about your account.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the problem in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- I'm back from my trip and want to un-pause my subscription"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal subscription ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your subscription and find it.
- Make it clear you want to resume / reactivate the existing paused subscription, not start a brand-new one.
- Let the agent do the work of looking up your account and your subscription. Don't hand it everything in the first message.
- If the agent offers to change anything else, politely decline -- just resume the subscription.
- Once the agent confirms your subscription is active again, you're satisfied and done.
